D9 Biology L2 Understand Reference

Why Are Leaves Green — The Green Paradox

the sun peaks at green-yellow yet plants reflect those wavelengths; the puzzle isn't “why green” but “why don't plants absorb the brightest band”

A naive observer expects plants to absorb the spectrum where the sun is brightest — green/yellow. Yet they reflect green. The cleanest hypothesis (Marosvölgyi & van Gorkom 2010): plants live in a fluctuating-light world; absorbing the brightest band would over-excite the reaction centres at peak flux and damage them. By skipping the peak, photosynthesis is more stable across the day.

Toggle the “naive” vs “real” absorption strategy. Naive absorbs the solar peak (would damage); real misses the peak (stable). Both overlay on the AM1.5 solar spectrum.
